Title: C-17 Globemaster III Sustainment Contract (G3) - Phase II, Ordering Period 6 & 7 Landing Gear
Awardee: THE BOEING COMPANY Long Beach CA 90808 USA
Description: This statement provides notice that the government has awarded the Phase II modification of an unpriced option for sustainment services in support of the C-17 Globemaster Ill worldwide fleet. On 28 Sept 21, a 9-year, 7-month Indefinite Delivery, Indefinite Quantity (IDIQ) contract was awarded to acquire sustainment services for the worldwide sustainment and logistical support of the C-17 Globemaster Ill fleet under contract FA8526-21-D-0001. This contract includes FAR 52.217-8 and FAR 52.217-9 options. It includes Fixed-Price-Incentive (Firm Target) (FPIF), Firm-Fixed Price (FFP), CostPlus-Fixed-Fee (CPFF), Cost-Plus- Incentive-Fee (CPIF), and Time and Material (T&M) line items. The location of the work is CONUS and OCONUS and in multiple locations. The maximum value is $23,764,751,000. Support services provided for under this contract include Program and Project Management, Field Services, System and Sustaining Engineering, Lab Sustainment, Engine Sustainment, Aircraft Integrity Program Support, Technical Orders, Supply Chain Management, Material Improvement Projects, Obsolescence Services, Software Design, Development, and Testing, and Depot Maintenance and Modifications. The contract was awarded with three phases of ordering periods: [1] "Phase I" (Basic Ordering Period 1, Ordering Period 2, and Ordering Period 3), [2] "Option - Phase II" (Ordering Period 4, Ordering Period 5, Ordering Period 6 and Ordering Period 7), and [3] "Option - Phase Ill". Phase I was included in the initial award. Option Phase II was awarded and effective 01 October 2024. Option Phase Ill was included at award but has not yet been exercised. This modification is hereto applicable and effective to Ordering Periods 6 & 7 Material only and is specific to the Landing Gear Spares support required during this period of performance. This modification is not retroactive. As provided in the redacted synopsized Justification and Approval (J&A) on or about 13 Oct 2021 on SAM.gov, this contract was awarded using Other Than Full and Open Competition prescribed by FAR 6.302-1 "Only One Responsible Source and No Other Supplies or Services Will Satisfy Agency Requirements". The contract is a sole source award to The Boeing Company (CAGE: 88277).

Title: Propane Gas and Related Services IDIQ
Awardee: Lunar Holdings LLC Fairfax VA 22030 USA
Description: The Department of the Air Force has awarded a 5-year Indefinite Delivery/Indefinite Quantity (IDIQ) contract to Lunar Holdings LLC for propane delivery, tank maintenance, and related services at Beale Air Force Base, California. Under this contract, the contractor will provide all personnel, equipment, tools, materials, supervision, and services necessary for the following: Propane Delivery: Timely and reliable delivery of propane to government-owned tanks ranging in capacity from 500 to 10,000 gallons, ensuring uninterrupted operations. Tank Maintenance: Preventive maintenance and on-call repair services for government-owned propane tanks and associated systems, ensuring compliance with applicable safety standards and regulations. Tank Purchase and Installation: Delivery and installation of new propane tanks. This contract includes a firm-fixed delivery fee with an Economic Price Adjustment (EPA) for the propane commodity, based on the Butane-Propane News (BPN) index for the San Joaquin, CA region. The total contract ceiling is $3.4 million, with a maximum annual ordering limitation of $690,000. The contract will include a 5-year ordering period, during which the Government may issue task orders on an as-needed basis. This contract was awarded through a competitive Women-Owned Small Business (WOSB) set-aside. Lunar Holdings LLC was selected as the awardee following a competitive evaluation process. The contract has a total ordering period of five (5) years, commencing on 8 April 2026 and ending on 7 April 2031.

Title: V212--36C250-26-AP-0790 - 36C25026P0538 - TRANSPORTATION SERVICE - BEACHWOOD TRANSPORTATION - 5/1/2026 to 4/30/2027 - BASE YEAR - 541D60003
Description: The Department of Veterans Affairs, Network Contracting Office (NCO) 10, has awarded a firm fixed price purchase order for non emergent ambulatory and wheelchair/ambulette transportation services for research participants at the Louis Stokes Cleveland VA Medical Center, Cleveland, OH. Contract Number: 36C25026P0538 Awardee: Beachwood Transportation LLC (UEI: S5MUDWPEM8G7) Type of Contract: Firm fixed price, commercial services NAICS: 485991 Special Needs Transportation (total small business set aside) Source Selection:Â Lowest Priced Technically Acceptable (LPTA)Â under FAR Part 12 and Part 13 procedures. Total Estimated Value (Base + All Options): $118,900.00 Base Period of Performance: 05/01/2026 04/30/2027 Option Periods: Three option years, if exercised, through 04/30/2030. Services include round trip, non emergent transportation for eligible VA research participants, both ambulatory and wheelchair/ambulette, within and outside defined mileage bands of the medical center, ordered on an as needed basis and billed at firm fixed unit prices per trip. Estimated historical usage is approximately 270 round trips per year; however, quantities are estimates only and do not represent a guaranteed minimum. In accordance with FAR 13.106 3, individual written debriefings or detailed statements of reasons for non selection are not required for this simplified acquisition; unsuccessful vendors may request only a brief explanation of the basis for award. Award was made as a 100% small business set aside.

Title: Tri-Service CAD/PAD Life Cycle Management Support
Awardee: MARTIN-BAKER AIRCRAFT COMPANY LIMITED Uxbridge UB9 5AJ GBR
Description: The Naval Surface Warfare Center Indian Head Division (NSWC IHD) awarded a firm-fixed price, Basic Ordering Agreement on 10 April 2026, for Engineering Services to support Cartridge and Propellant Actuated Devices for the Joint Program Office requirements. The services under this agreement cover technology, development, product, improvement, qualification and prototyping support, quality evaluation and surveillance program support. As a result of N0017425SN0014, the Government awarded a Shared Ceiling Firm-Fixed Price agreement in the amount of $18,192,118.70 to Martin Baker Aircraft Company Ltd, Uxbridge, UB9 5AJ, GBR.

Title: Military OneSource Program - Request for Proposal (RFP) 47QFCA23R0053 Reprocurement Award Notice
Description: The contract (47QFCA26C0001) resulting from solicitation number 47QFCA23R0053 and the termination of Contract 47QFCA25C0002 was awarded on April 17, 2026, to Leidos, Inc. for $456,277,600. This competitively awarded contract to Leidos, Inc. is in support of the Military OneSource Program for military members and their families, similar to a commercial Employee Assistance Program (EAP). This program provides members of the Armed Forces and their families, about 4.7 million persons or “participants” at locations worldwide, with a broad array of information and referrals to both military and civilian resources as well as counseling services. Through one centralized source, the services provided by Military OneSource are available 24 hours a day, seven days a week (24/7), via the Internet, telephone (i.e., 800 number and collect calls), electronic mail (e-mail), postal mail, secure real-time messaging (i.e.,chat), Artificial Intelligence (AI) chatbot, and, if requested, face-to-face counseling. This is a dynamic environment encompassing comprehensive support systems related to military members and their families.
